title: Hedyotis remyi
intro: Hedyotis remyi, also known as 'Ohia lehua', is a type of flowering plant native to Hawaii and other parts of the Pacific Islands. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Hedyotis remyi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as a topical ointment or salve, to harness its therapeutic benefits.

This family is part of the larger order Gentianales and the class Magnoliopsida. The plant's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants and its unique characteristics.
